title: ConsolidationReceipt (v1)
description: Signed structural proof of a single consolidation cycle's output. The motebit's Ed25519 identity key commits to counts of memories merged / promoted / pruned, the cycle's phase list, and timestamps. Self-verifiable from the receipt bytes + the signer's public key alone — no relay, no billing relationship. The privacy boundary is the type: no field carries memory content. See spec/consolidation-receipt-v1.md §3.

receipt_id.description: UUIDv4 — the receipt's own identity. Distinct from `cycle_id`: a cycle may (in future versions) produce multiple receipts, and receipts outlive their cycle in the audit log.

motebit_id.description: Signer's MotebitId. Also the Solana address when anchored (Ed25519 coincidence).

public_key.description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 public key of the signer. Embedded for portable verification — third parties verify without a registry lookup. Optional for backward-compat; new receipts SHOULD include it.

cycle_id.description: Matches the `cycle_id` carried by the `consolidation_cycle_run` event the runtime emits at cycle completion. Verifiers cross-reference to correlate receipts with audit events.

started_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the cycle's first phase began. Signed — a verifier cannot backdate a receipt without breaking the signature.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the cycle's last phase completed or yielded. `finished_at >= started_at` is a semantic invariant verifiers MAY check.

items.description: One of the four consolidation-cycle phases. Closed union: adding a phase is a protocol-coordinated change (new enum value + new wire version).

phases_run.description: Ordered list of phases that ran to completion (or yielded with partial work). Lets verifiers scope a receipt to specific work (e.g., a prune-only cycle vs. a full orient → gather → consolidate → prune).

items.description: One of the four consolidation-cycle phases. Closed union: adding a phase is a protocol-coordinated change (new enum value + new wire version).

phases_yielded.description: Subset of `phases_run` — phases whose AbortSignal fired mid-execution (budget exhausted or parent signal aborted). Lets verifiers distinguish fully-completed cycles from budget-clipped ones.

orient_nodes.description: Count of nodes touched during the orient phase. Structural count only — never a node identifier or content.

gather_clusters.description: Count of episodic-memory clusters identified during gather.

gather_notable.description: Count of memories ranked notable for reflection during gather.

consolidate_merged.description: Count of cluster-merges produced during consolidate (each merge tombstones N ≥ 2 episodic memories and forms one semantic memory).

pruned_decay.description: Count of memories tombstoned during prune due to decayed-confidence threshold.

pruned_notability.description: Count of memories tombstoned during prune due to low-notability threshold (isolated, low-confidence, low-relevance).

pruned_retention.description: Count of memories tombstoned during prune due to sensitivity-level retention policy (privacy-layer enforced).

summary.description: Structural counts. Closed field set — the privacy boundary is the type. A v2 receipt adding a field that could carry memory content, embeddings, or sensitive identifiers is a protocol break; interoperable evolution is additive-counts-only.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for consolidation receipts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature. Signed body is `canonicalJson(receipt_without_signature)`. Verifiers reconstruct canonical bytes from all fields except `signature`, encode to UTF-8, run Ed25519.verify against `public_key` (or a looked-up key).
